title: STANDARD FOR COCOA BUTTER CXS 86-1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 2001. Amended in 2016, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR COCOA BUTTER CXS 86-1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 2001. Amended in 2016, 2022.
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Cocoa Butter (CXS 86-1981), adopted 1981, revised 2001, amended 2016 and 2022.
Scope: applies exclusively to cocoa butter used as an ingredient in the manufacture of chocolate and chocolate products.
Section heading only (Description) — no substantive provisions in this entry.

Defines cocoa butter and sets compositional limits: free fatty acids (as oleic acid) not more than 1.75% m/m; unsaponifiable matter not more than 0.7% m/m, except press cocoa butter where it shall not exceed 0.35% m/m.
Food additives: no additives permitted; processing aids must be consistent with CXG 75-2010 and hexane (62–82 °C) residues are limited to 1 mg/kg (excluding press cocoa butter).
Processing aid provision: processing aids should follow CXG 75-2010 and hexane (62–82 °C) is limited to a maximum of 1 mg/kg, excluding press cocoa butter.
Maximum level specification: hexane (62–82 °C) residues are limited to 1 mg/kg in cocoa butter, with press cocoa butter excluded, and processing aids must align with CXG 75-2010.

Hygiene recommendation: products should be prepared and handled per the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and must comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Labelling requirements: in addition to CXS 1-1985, the product name shall be 'Cocoa Butter' (or 'Press Cocoa Butter' for products meeting Section 2.1) and labelling of non-retail containers should follow CXS 346-2021.
Name of the product: the product must be labelled 'Cocoa Butter' except that 'Press Cocoa Butter' may be used for products meeting the Section 2.1 description.
Labelling provisions (same as 5): reiterates that product naming and non-retail container labelling must comply with the General Standard for Labelling (CXS 1-1985) and CXS 346-2021 respectively.
Non-retail container labelling: labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Section heading only (Methods of Analysis and Sampling) — no substantive methods listed in this entry.
Analytical method for free fatty acids: determination shall be performed according to IUPAC (1987) method 2.201.
Analytical method for unsaponifiable matter: determination shall be performed according to IUPAC (1987) method 2.401.
Analytical method for lead: determination shall be performed according to AOAC 934.07 or the referenced IUPAC method (Pure & Appl. Chem., 63).
